concept.name: grid-column start and end
concept.explain:
The 'grid-column-start' property specifies on which column-line the grid item will start.
The 'grid-column-end' property specifies on which column-line the grid item will end.
The 'grid-column' property is a shorthand property for the grid-column-start and the grid-column-end properties.
Use these properties to move the car to the targeted parking spot and make it fit exactly.
concept.name: grid-column

ui.prompt: Goal: Move the car horizontally into the highlighted parking spot.
hints: Use grid-column to move the car horizontally.
You need to move from column 3 to column 4.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200,0,0,0.22); outline:2px dashed rgba(200,0,0,0.8); border-radius:10px;

concept.name: grid-row start and end
concept.explain:
The 'grid-row-start' property specifies on which row-line the grid item will start.
The 'grid-row-end' property specifies on which row-line the grid item will end.
The 'grid-row' property is a shorthand property for the grid-row-start and the grid-row-end properties.
Use these properties to move the car to the targeted parking spot and make it fit exactly.
concept.name: grid-row

ui.prompt: Goal: Move the car vertically so it fills the highlighted parking spot.
hints: Use grid-row to move the car vertically.
The spot starts at row 3 and ends at row 6.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200,0,0,0.22); outline:2px dashed rgba(200,0,0,0.8); border-radius:10px;

concept.name: combine grid-column and grid-row
concept.explain:
This spot is larger in both directions.
Combine 'grid-column' with 'grid-row' so the car matches the target exactly.
concept.name: grid-column + grid-row
concept.explain:
Grid items can be positioned on both axes using grid-column and grid-row.

Use both properties together to place an item anywhere in the grid.

ui.prompt: Goal: Park the car so it covers the entire 2×2 highlighted space.
hints: You must move the car horizontally and vertically.
Look at the grid lines around the parking spot.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200,0,0,0.22); outline:2px dashed rgba(200,0,0,0.8); border-radius:10px;

concept.name: span
concept.explain:
The span keyword lets an item stretch across multiple grid tracks.

Example:
grid-column: 3 / span 2;

This starts at column line 3 and spans across 2 columns.

ui.prompt: Goal: Start the car at column 3 and make it cover two parking spaces using span.
hints: Use span to stretch the car across columns.
The car should start at column 3.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200,0,0,0.22); outline:2px dashed rgba(200,0,0,0.8); border-radius:10px;
